capacity_evidence.device: single Blackhole chip selected through a 1x1 mesh
capacity_evidence.probe: tests/functional_decoder_capacity_probe.py executes one complete prefill, including dense 32-expert MoE, and synchronizes the output back to the host
capacity_evidence.first_failure_operation: MoE up-value ttnn.slice after dense gate/up projection
capacity_evidence.full_context_formula: 32 experts * 131072 tokens * 5760 gate/up values * 2 BF16 bytes
capacity_evidence.boundary_note: The exact logical length 21249 pads to 21280 for TILE storage. It is the first length above 21248 and fails because its requested per-bank allocation exceeds the largest contiguous free block. The constructor now rejects lengths above the measured boundary before allocating weights.

notes: The optimized decoder preserves the inherited public maximum of 21248 and the BF16 linear KV-cache shape/dtype, so advertised capacity is not reduced. Routed BFP8 sparse experts materially lower decoder working memory versus the functional dense all-expert path, but this stage is constrained to optimized_decoder.py and cannot raise the inherited SUPPORTED_CONTEXT constructor contract without changing the completed functional decoder. Extending and re-probing that public contract is therefore deferred rather than advertising an unvalidated larger length.

prefill.optimized_capacity_status: preserved from the functional contract; sparse expert working memory is lower but no larger public length is advertised
prefill.cache_behavior: fills the linear key/value cache from position zero

decode.cache_behavior: updates the persistent linear key/value cache in place and runs decode SDPA
